title: Employee Travel and Expense Reimbursement Policy
content:
Chapter 1 General Provisions
Article 1 This policy standardizes company travel and expense reimbursement workflows to ensure cost control and audit traceability.
Article 2 This policy applies to all full-time employees and approved contractors.

Chapter 2 Reimbursable Scope
Article 3 Reimbursable expenses include transportation, accommodation, meals, office purchases, and pre-approved client reception expenses.
Article 4 Non-reimbursable expenses include personal entertainment, over-standard accommodation, and unapproved gift purchases.

Chapter 3 Approval Rules
Article 5 A single reimbursement not exceeding 1000 CNY requires direct manager approval.
Article 6 A single reimbursement above 1000 CNY and up to 5000 CNY requires two-level approval by direct manager and department head.
Article 7 A single reimbursement above 5000 CNY requires three-level approval by direct manager, department head, and finance head.

Chapter 4 Timeliness
Article 8 Employees must submit reimbursement requests within 30 calendar days after expense occurrence.
Article 9 Finance must complete review and payment within 5 business days after all required documents are provided.

title: Employee Leave and Time Off Policy
content:
Chapter 1 Scope
Article 1 This policy applies to all full-time employees.

Chapter 2 Leave Types
Article 2 Leave types include personal leave, sick leave, annual leave, marriage leave, maternity leave, and paternity leave.
Article 3 Sick leave longer than three consecutive days requires a certificate from a qualified medical institution.

Chapter 3 Approval Requirements
Article 4 Leave up to half a day requires direct manager approval.
Article 5 Leave above one day and up to three days requires direct manager and department head approval.
Article 6 Leave longer than three days requires HR filing and department head approval.

title: Attendance and Clock In Management Standard
content:
Article 1 The company applies standard workday attendance from 09:00 to 18:00.
Article 2 Employees must complete one clock in before work and one clock out after work.
Article 3 More than three late arrivals or early leaves in one month require a manager counseling session.
Article 4 Employees on field duty who cannot clock in must submit a field attendance correction request on the same day with travel evidence.

title: Domestic Business Travel Policy
content:
Chapter 1 Travel Request
Article 1 Employees must submit travel requests at least two business days in advance.
Article 2 Travel requests must include destination, itinerary, budget, and client visit objectives.

Chapter 2 Transport and Hotel Standards
Article 3 Default train class is second class and default flight class is economy class.
Article 4 Hotel limit is 500 CNY per night in tier-1 cities and 350 CNY per night in other cities.

Chapter 3 Reimbursement
Article 5 Travel expenses must be reimbursed within ten business days after trip completion.

title: Office Procurement and Acceptance Procedure
content:
Article 1 Office procurement must follow request first, purchase second, and acceptance third.
Article 2 A single procurement not exceeding 3000 CNY requires department head approval.
Article 3 A single procurement above 3000 CNY requires joint approval by administration and finance.
Article 4 Delivered goods must be accepted within two business days and acceptance records must be uploaded to the system.

title: Contract Approval and Archiving Policy
content:
Chapter 1 Approval Flow
Article 1 Business contracts must be reviewed by the initiating department, legal, and finance in sequence.
Article 2 Contracts exceeding 500000 CNY require final approval from the general manager.

Chapter 2 Archiving Requirements
Article 3 Effective contracts must be electronically archived within three business days.
Article 4 Original paper contracts are centrally managed by legal and cannot be borrowed without approval.

title: Account and Password Security Standard
content:
Article 1 Employee accounts are personal and must not be shared.
Article 2 Password length must be at least 12 characters and include uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and special symbols.
Article 3 Passwords for critical systems must be changed at least every 90 days.
Article 4 Accounts are automatically locked for 30 minutes after five consecutive failed login attempts.

title: Data Classification and Handling Policy
content:
Article 1 Company data is classified into public, internal, sensitive, and core levels.
Article 2 Sensitive and core data must not be sent through personal email or consumer messaging tools.
Article 3 Access to sensitive data requires owner approval and access log recording.
Article 4 Data exports involving customer privacy must be desensitized before transmission.

title: System Access Request and Revocation Policy
content:
Article 1 Access requests follow the principle of least privilege.
Article 2 New access requests must describe business scenario, access scope, and expiration date.
Article 3 Access must be revoked within one business day when an employee leaves or changes role.
Article 4 High-risk privileged accounts must enable multi-factor authentication.

title: Production Release and Change Management Process
content:
Article 1 Production release requires a change request with code review and test evidence.
Article 2 Major changes should be executed during low traffic windows and must include rollback plans.
Article 3 Core service indicators must be checked within 30 minutes after release.
Article 4 For a P1 incident, the change owner must join the emergency response channel within 10 minutes.

title: Incident Response and Escalation Mechanism
content:
Article 1 Incident severity levels are P1, P2, and P3.
Article 2 A P1 incident must be confirmed within 5 minutes and emergency procedures must start immediately.
Article 3 A P2 incident requires initial diagnosis within 15 minutes and stakeholder notification.
Article 4 A postmortem report with root cause and corrective actions is required within 24 hours after recovery.

title: Meeting Management and Minutes Standard
content:
Article 1 Meeting organizers must send agenda and attendee list at least one day in advance.
Article 2 Meetings longer than 60 minutes must define phase goals and time allocation.
Article 3 Meeting minutes must be distributed within 24 hours and include decisions, owners, and due dates.
Article 4 Weekly meetings must track completion status of previous action items.

title: Overtime and Comp Time Policy
content:
Article 1 Overtime must be based on business necessity and approved in advance.
Article 2 Weekday overtime should be compensated with comp time within three months.
Article 3 Holiday overtime may be compensated according to labor regulations by overtime pay or comp time.
Article 4 Unapproved overtime is not counted in official time records.

title: Fixed Asset Check Out and Return Policy
content:
Article 1 Asset check out for laptops, monitors, and related equipment must be registered in the asset system.
Article 2 Asset borrowing requires department head approval and a defined return deadline.
Article 3 All assets must be returned and verified before employee offboarding.
Article 4 Asset loss must be reported within 24 hours with follow-up investigation support.

title: Vendor Onboarding and Management Standard
content:
Article 1 New vendor onboarding requires qualification review, compliance review, and pricing assessment.
Article 2 Projects above 100000 CNY should compare proposals from at least three vendors.
Article 3 Vendor evaluation is performed every six months across quality, delivery, service, and compliance.
Article 4 Vendors with two consecutive failed evaluations must be suspended.

title: Business Reception and Gift Management Policy
content:
Article 1 Business reception must follow necessity, cost discipline, and compliance principles.
Article 2 A single reception budget above 2000 CNY requires pre-approval.
Article 3 Reception reports and expense details must be submitted within five business days after the event.
Article 4 Gift purchases must record purpose, amount, and recipient and cannot be retained for personal use.

title: Invoice Management and Tax Compliance Standard
content:
Article 1 Reimbursement invoices must be authentic, lawful, and complete.
Article 2 Finance may reject invoices when the payer name or tax number is inconsistent with company registration information.
Article 3 Electronic invoices require original files and verification evidence.
Article 4 Reimbursement involving VAT special invoices must be submitted within certification deadlines.

title: Training Request and Learning Expense Reimbursement Policy
content:
Article 1 External training participation requires advance approval with expected business value.
Article 2 Training reimbursement requires course notice, payment receipt, and learning summary.
Article 3 A single training cost above 8000 CNY requires division head approval.
Article 4 Employees sponsored by company training must deliver internal knowledge sharing after completion.

title: Remote Work Management Guideline
content:
Article 1 Remote work requires direct manager approval and system registration.
Article 2 During remote work, employees must remain reachable and attend required meetings.
Article 3 Operations involving sensitive data must use company VPN and managed devices.
Article 4 Continuous remote work above five business days requires periodic progress reporting.

title: Document Archiving and Version Management Standard
content:
Article 1 Policy documents must include version number, effective date, and publishing department.
Article 2 Revised documents must include revision notes with additions, modifications, and deprecated clauses.
Article 3 Historical versions must be retained for at least three years for audit purposes.
Article 4 Archive directories should be organized by department and topic to ensure traceability and discoverability.

title: Employee Directory Source-of-Truth Policy
content:
Chapter 1 Source of Truth
Article 1 The structured employee directory in the rag_employees PostgreSQL table is the single source of truth for employee identity, department, job title, employee_id, and corporate email.
Article 2 Free-text policy documents must not contradict the structured directory; when conflict occurs, the structured record wins.

Chapter 2 Required Lookup Behavior
Article 3 Any question that asks who an employee is, who works in a department, who holds a specific job title, or who to contact must trigger a structured directory lookup before any narrative answer is produced.
Article 4 The lookup must perform a case-insensitive substring match across name, department, title, employee_id and email.

Chapter 3 Empty-Result Handling
Article 5 If the directory returns zero matching records the assistant must clearly tell the user that no matching employee was found and must not invent a name, department, title or email.
Article 6 The assistant must not guess an employee's manager, location, or contact details from policy text alone.

title: Employee Question Answering Standard
content:
Chapter 1 Grounding Rules
Article 1 When the user asks about an individual employee, the assistant must rely only on records returned from the employee directory and may not synthesize identity fields from prose policies.
Article 2 When the user asks who works in a department or which employees hold a job title, the assistant must enumerate exact directory matches and explicitly state the count.

Chapter 2 Response Templates
Article 3 When at least one record is returned, respond with: name, employee_id, department, title, and email - in that order - for each matching employee.
Article 4 When zero records are returned, respond with the fixed phrase 'no matching employee was found in the directory' and offer to refine the search by department or title.
Article 5 The assistant must never reveal records that were not returned by the structured lookup, even if they appear in unrelated narrative documents.

Chapter 3 Privacy and Compliance
Article 6 The assistant must not disclose home address, salary, performance rating, or any field outside the directory schema (employee_id, name, department, title, email).
Article 7 Bulk listings of more than ten employees in a single response require an explicit confirmation step from the user.

title: Departments and Job Title Taxonomy
content:
Chapter 1 Standard Departments
Article 1 The recognised top-level departments in the directory are Engineering, Information Technology, Human Resources, Finance, Sales, Legal, and Administration.
Article 2 Department names must be matched case-insensitively; common abbreviations such as 'HR' for 'Human Resources' or 'IT' for 'Information Technology' are accepted as input but normalised to the full form when answering.

Chapter 2 Job Title Conventions
Article 3 Engineering job titles include Backend Engineer, Frontend Engineer, Senior Backend Engineer, Engineering Manager, and Site Reliability Engineer.
Article 4 Human Resources job titles include HR Business Partner, Recruiter, and Compensation Specialist.
Article 5 Finance job titles include Finance Lead, Financial Analyst, and Accounts Payable Specialist.
Article 6 Sales job titles include Account Executive, Sales Development Representative, and Regional Sales Manager.

Chapter 3 Cross-Reference Rules
Article 7 When the user mentions a synonym or abbreviation for a department or title, the assistant must attempt the structured lookup with both the synonym and the canonical form before declaring no match.
